Article Title: Twisha Sharma death case: Cops take custody of husband, will be taken to Bhopal
Article Text:
Samarth Singh at Jabalpur court (ANI)

Also Read |

Also Read |

(With agency inputs)





NEW DELHI: Samarth Singh, the husband of Twisha Sharma, a model-turned-actor from Noida who was found dead at her matrimonial home in Bhopal, on Friday appeared at a Jabalpur court to surrender, his lawyer said.Later, the Jabalpur Police took his custody and took him to the nearby Omti police station. He would be handed over to Bhopal police,This came after Singh reached the district court to surrender after withdrawing his pre-arrest bail application filed in the Madhya Pradesh high court.A lawyer by profession, he had been evading arrest since an FIR was registered against him and his mother, former district judge Giribala Singh, following Twish's death on May 12.Members of Twisha's family and their lawyers were also present outside the court.Her mother--in-law Giribala Singh — a retired district and sessions judge who currently heads the Bhopal Consumer Court — was granted anticipatory bail by a city court last week.Twisha Sharma was found dead at her matrimonial home in the state capital’s Katara Hills area. While her family accused her in-laws of dowry harassment and abetment to suicide, the Singh family alleged that she was addicted to drugs.Police registered an FIR against Samarth Singh and his mother under Sections 80(2), 85 and 3(5) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, along with relevant provisions of the Dowry Prohibition Act.Police had also announced a cash reward of Rs 30,000 for information leading to Samarth Singh’s arrest and moved court seeking the revocation of his passport.
Article Summary: Samarth Singh, the husband of Twisha Sharma, was found dead at her matrimonial home in Bhopal. He had been evading arrest since an FIR was registered against him and his mother, former district judge Giribala Singh, following Twish's death.

Article Title: Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats on June 18; Mallikarjun Kharge, HD Deve Gowda set to retire
Article Text: New Delhi: Congress is set to go unrepresented from Gujarat in Rajya Sabha for the first time as Election Commission announced polls for 24 seats in the House on June 18. BJP's two minority faces in govt and Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge and former PM H D Deve Gowda figure among those retiring from Upper House.Going by current strength of state assemblies, where RS elections will be held on June 18, Congress may gain at least one seat and governing NDA will sweep all four seats in Andhra Pradesh amid possibility of an intriguing contest in at least two states, Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh, if BJP chooses to do so.The 24 seats are spread across Andhra Pradesh (4), Gujarat (4), Karnataka (4), Madhya Pradesh (3), Rajasthan (3), Jharkhand (2) and one vacancy each in four northeast states of Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya and Mizoram.With Shaktisinh Gohil, the lone Congress MP in RS from Gujarat, retiring and his party lacking numbers to retain the seat, the BJP bastion will no longer have any member from the rival party in RS.Against the 12 outgoing seats, BJP is all but certain to retain 11 and can bag even more if an ally like TDP backs its nominee or if a few members of rival parties support its nominees, a political manoeuvring it has often pulled off in the past.Union ministers Ravneet Singh 'Bittu' and George Kurian, BJP's Sikh and Christian faces from Punjab and Kerala respectively, will retire from RS next month. Bittu was elected from Rajasthan and Kurian from MP as BJP lacked requisite strength in assemblies of their home states.JD(S) leader Deve Gowda, who is 93, has indicated that he will not be coming back to RS and the numbers in Karnataka assembly anyway suggest that BJP-JD(S) alliance can win only one seat against the three it currently has. Kharge (83), too, is among the retiring members from the state.While YSR Congress has three outgoing MPs, its rout in the 2024 assembly polls means that it cannot win any seat.In Jharkhand, NDA is four short of 28 MLAs needed to win one seat.In MP where former CM Digvijaya Singh is among the three retiring MPs, Congress has 64 MLAs against the 58 needed to win one seat. BJP, which will have nearly 48 votes to spare after retaining its two seats, can force a contest.
Article Summary: Election Commission announced polls for 24 seats in the House on June 18. BJP's two minority faces in govt and Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge and former PM H D Deve Gowda figure among those retiring from Upper House. Against the 12 outgoing seats, BJP is all but certain to retain 11.

Article Title: Rajya Sabha polls on June 18: NDA may lose seat, Congress set to gain; TVK debut likely
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The BJP-led NDA is likely to lose a seat in the Rajya Sabha while the Congress-led opposition could improve its tally in the upcoming biennial elections for 26 seats across 12 states.The Election Commission on Friday announced that polling for the Rajya Sabha seats will be held on June 18. The seats are falling vacant due to the retirement of incumbent members between June 21 and July 19, including former prime minister HD Deve Gowda, Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, senior Congress leader Digvijaya Singh, and Union ministers Ravneet Singh and George Kurian.Elections will be held for four seats each in Andhra Pradesh, Gujarat and Karnataka; three each in Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan; two in Jharkhand; and one each in Manipur, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh and Mizoram. Bypolls for one seat each in Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu will also be held the same day.Of the 26 seats going to polls, the NDA currently holds 18, the Congress four, the Jharkhand Mukti Morcha (JMM) one and the YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) three.However, based on current assembly numbers, the NDA is expected to secure 17 seats, while the Congress may improve its tally to five seats. The JMM is likely to win two seats, while Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is projected to bag one seat in Tamil Nadu, according to the PTI report.The NDA currently has 149 MPs in the 244-member Rajya Sabha, while the opposition has 78 members and non-aligned regional parties hold 17 seats.The Election Commission has fixed June 8 as the last date for filing nominations.The bypoll in Tamil Nadu was necessitated after AIADMK leader C Ve Shanmugam resigned following his election to the state assembly. Going by current numbers, Vijay-led TVK is expected to win the seat.In Maharashtra, the bypoll is being held following the resignation of NCP leader Sunetra Pawar after her election to the state assembly. The NDA is expected to retain the seat.In Karnataka, the Congress is projected to win three of the four Rajya Sabha seats, with the BJP likely to secure one. In Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan, the BJP is expected to win two seats each while the Congress may get one seat in both states.In Andhra Pradesh, the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) is likely to sweep all four seats, while the BJP is expected to win all four seats in Gujarat.In Jharkhand, the JMM-Congress alliance is likely to secure both seats, though the BJP may attempt to win one through cross-voting support.
Article Summary: The Election Commission on Friday announced that polling for the Rajya Sabha seats will be held on June 18. The seats are falling vacant due to the retirement of incumbent members between June 21 and July 19. Of the 26 seats going to polls, the NDA currently holds 18, the Congress four, the Jharkhand Mukti Morcha one.

Article Title: ‘I’ll tell you who received how much’: Harbhajan Singh’s political ‘doosra’, claims AAP sold Rajya Sabha seat
Article Text:
Former cricketer Harbhajan Singh has accused the Aam Aadmi Party of selling a Rajya Sabha seat from Punjab.





JALANDHAR: Former India off-spinner and current Rajya Sabha member Harbhajan Singh , who crossed over from Aam Aadmi Party to BJP along with five other RS members from Punjab, spun a doosra at AAP on Friday, alleging that an RS seat from Punjab was ‘sold'.Harbhajan, who has already restricted who can respond to his posts, was reacting to an X user's taunt on why he hadn't resigned from the RS seat ‘given' to him and what was his compulsion to join BJP. "What price did you put on your conscience? I have heard that BJP has bid 25-25 crore for traitors — is it true?" the X user asked.Harbhajan shot back: "When the time comes, every statement of yours will be answered. And I did not abuse any of your leaders. Why would I dirty my own tongue? And to those calling me a traitor, first ask your own people at what price the Rajya Sabha seat of Punjab was sold. If they don't tell you, I will tell you who received how much offering and from whose side.And how people were made ministers and officials to loot Punjab and deliver goods to the ‘Lala'. They have looted and finished Punjab."Earlier, in an apparent dig at AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal following a dig from an AAP supporter, Harbhajan had remarked: "...Iwish your leader had 1% affection with Punjab...."Harbhajan's reply was immediately lapped up by Congress functionaries, who urged him to make more ‘disclosures'."Harbhajan Singh's statement has exposed the ugly truth. The people of Punjab deserve answers from Arvind Kejriwal and Bhagwant Mann," said Leader of Opposition Partap Singh Bajwa. Congress MLA Sukhpal Singh Khaira dared Kejriwal and CM Bhagwant Mann to accept Harbhajan's challenge. Congress MLA Pargat Singh demanded clarifications from both AAP and BJP. They alleged that people of Punjab "already knew that RS seats were sold".
Article Summary: Harbhajan Singh has accused the Aam Aadmi Party of selling a Rajya Sabha seat from Punjab. The former India off-spinner crossed over to the BJP along with five other RS members. Congress functionaries urged him to make more ‘disclosures'

Article Title: Lucknow doctor arrested for raping Class 12th student after giving sedative injection inside OT
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





LUCKNOW: A 30-year-old doctor was arrested for allegedly raping a 20-year-old woman inside the operation theatre of a private hospital in Lucknow.The health department sealed the hospital and initiated proceedings for the suspension of the doctor’s licence following the intervention of deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak.The incident came to light from a private hospital in Indaurabagh area of Bakshi-ka-Talab in Lucknow, where the patient was admitted for treatment of epileptic seizures.Police said the accused, Dr Vijay Kumar Giri, allegedly raped the student after administering a sedative injection to her inside the operation theatre on May 21.According to the survivor’s father, his daughter was admitted to the hospital on May 19 for treatment of recurring epilepsy-related complications.He said that around 3 pm on May 21, his daughter developed breathing difficulty, after which Dr Giri took her to the operation theatre on the pretext of administering oxygen support.The patient’s elder sister and a nurse initially accompanied her inside the OT, but the doctor asked both to step outside after a few minutes.In his complaint to police, the survivor’s father alleged that the doctor injected the girl with an anaesthetic through the intravenous cannula attached to her hand, after which she lost consciousness.According to the survivor’s statement to the police, she regained partial consciousness sometime later and ran out of the operation theatre crying, alleging that the doctor raped her. Police said the survivor had dropped school for a few years due to her condition and was currently studying in Class 12.ADCP (North) Twinkle Jain confirmed that an FIR for rape was lodged, adding that relevant sections of the SC/ST Act were also invoked.Deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak took cognisance and directed strict action against the hospital and the doctor.In a statement on social media, Pathak said, “The state govt stands firmly with the survivor and her family, and strict punishment to the accused will be ensured.”He directed officials to seal the hospital and suspend the medical licence of the accused.
Article Summary: Dr Vijay Kumar Giri, 30, allegedly raped the student after administering a sedative injection to her inside the operation theatre on May 21. The health department sealed the hospital and initiated proceedings for the suspension of the doctor’s licence. Deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak took cognisance and directed strict action against the hospital.

Article Title: Falta votes 'without fear' for first time in years, records 88% turnout
Article Text:
.





FALTA/KOLKATA: Bengal's Falta assembly constituency clocked a high 87.9% turnout (till 8pm) in Thursday's repoll as voters, guarded by heavy CAPF deployment, spoke of casting ballots "without fear" for the first time in years, while TMC flags all but disappeared after party candidate Jahangir Khan withdrew from the contest 48 hours before voting.Polling remained peaceful, election officials said, a sharp contrast to April 29 when EC countermanded voting in Falta - one of seven assembly segments under the Diamond Harbour Lok Sabha seat represented by TMC's Abhishek Banerjee. It was the lone seat in Bengal where a repoll was ordered following allegations of widespread electoral malpractice, including booth capture, EVM tampering and intimidation. Falta had then registered a 91.8% turnout."High percentage of voting in Falta is unprecedented for a repoll. Usually, turnout in repolls dips. Here, voters responded to EC's participation campaign and turned up in a festive mood," Bengal additional CEO Dibyendu Das said. Counting is scheduled for Sunday.Across Falta, central forces outnumbered local political muscle. Voters repeatedly pointed to the absence of bikers and booth-side watchers, who they claimed had long shaped polling day equations."There are around 15 CAPF personnel here. Another 20-odd with two armoured Marksmen vehicles from J&K are standing on the main road. But more importantly, there are no bikers; there are no men keeping watch on you at the booth," voter Firoza Bibi said.The mood shift was starkest at Srirampur, Jahangir's home pocket, where brisk polling was seen under the near-total absence of TMC colours. Yet whispers of tactical voting persisted in minority pockets such as Gopalpur, Bapra and Rukhia, where residents spoke of quiet mobilisation behind an Independent candidate contesting on an Apple symbol. Turnout there remained subdued.For younger voters, the repoll carried symbolism beyond numbers. "For the first time since 2021, this election felt like a festival. Back then, bikers told me I was under watch and should not try to be adventurous," 23-year-old Alia Das said.Hashimnagar, a flashpoint of violence and unrest on April 29, saw turnout stay below 50% till afternoon. But residents spoke as though the verdict were already sealed. BJP candidate Debangshu Panda claimed anti-TMC sentiment had snowballed into a landslide.
Article Summary: Bengal's Falta assembly constituency clocked a high 87.9% turnout (till 8pm) in Thursday's repoll. Voters spoke of casting ballots "without fear" for the first time in years. TMC flags all but disappeared after party candidate Jahangir Khan withdrew.
